summary: Set 069 Session 2 of 6: trusted-command execution + diff-awareness + deeper probing -- the execution-evidence LANES wired into the automated pull-critique producer (rungs 2-3 of the proposal ladder). Ran on claude-opus-4-8 high (operator launch). This closes the literal 0.22.x gap the set exists for: the automated producer was a read-only commentator; it can now run operator-authored commands, see the raw diff, probe in bounded turns, and emit execution-backed evidence-tiered findings through the Session-1 protocol. ALL ADDITIVE -- absent the new config the loop + producer are byte-for-byte the Set 067/068 read-only behavior. SHIPPED CODE. ai_router/pull_verifier.py: (1) get_diff tool + DiffConfig (raw unified diff + changed-path list over an OPERATOR-PINNED ref range; the model passes NO args; dispatched to git DIRECTLY -- like run_test, OUTSIDE the byte-equality guard, because the orchestrator runs git itself with no model-touchable servant seam; raw ERROR on bad ref/timeout; recorded as a real probe). (2) _dispatch_run_test now returns a clean-run _Execution (command_id=the RESOLVED TRUSTED id via the new RunTestConfig.resolve_id, exit_code, raw_output) captured ONLY when ran AND no cage error AND no teardown leak. (3) the Session-1 evidence protocol WIRED: Finding gains evidence_tier+transcript (orchestrator-stamped, emitted only when set so an ASSERTED finding stays byte-identical); submit_verdict's evidenceTier/commandId proposal fields are GATED behind allow_evidence=(run_test_config is not None) so the read-only schema is the pre-069 shape; _stamp_evidence_tiers confers REPRODUCED ONLY via authoritative_tier on a transcript that validate_transcript accepts AND only after the orchestrator REPLAYS the named command on a SECOND pristine checkout whose outputHash matches (_run_pristine_replay) -- a claim with no matching run / unknown commandId / errored-or-leaked-or-mismatched replay COLLAPSES to a read-claim; HYPOTHESIS preserved; the meta-oracle holds by construction (entrypoint.kind=test_entrypoint, a trusted operator-authored command). ai_router/pull_critique.py: produce_path_aware_critique threads run_test_config/diff_config/caps to pull_route; budget_caps_for_paths derives blast-radius-budgeted PullCaps (required=1.0x, advisory=0.6x, none=0.4x with a 4-turn / 20k-token floor; max_output_tokens left intact) from the disposition files_changed when an execution lane is active and no caps are pinned (the 'turn/token caps per set, not a magic constant' lever); CLI flags --run-test-cmd/--run-test-named/--exec-ref/--exec-repo-root/--diff-base/--diff-head + _build_exec_configs (shlex-split, NO shell; --exec-ref required; malformed NAME=CMD rejected). DOC: ai_router/docs/pull-verifier.md gains a 'What Set 069 S2 added' section. VERIFICATION (cross-provider, gpt-5-4, gated->REQUIRED -- the 7-file ai_router+docs diff trips routed_gate: cross-artifact + multi-module + breadth; up-front conventions block per L-064-10). R1 FAIL with 3 REAL findings: F1 an UNKNOWN run_test name silently runs the DEFAULT command but was captured as the model's unmatched string, so a false commandId could back a REPRODUCED claim -> fixed via RunTestConfig.resolve_id capturing the resolved TRUSTED id; F2 the submit_verdict schema always advertised evidenceTier/commandId, changing the no-config agent-facing surface -> fixed by gating behind allow_evidence; F3 the unknown-commandId test fabricated an _Execution and never drove the real fallback -> added resolve_id unit + a real-cage {name:'missing'} dispatch test + a collapse test + verdict-schema-gating tests. (F8 doc accuracy was UNVERIFIED in R1 only because the doc edit post-dated `git add` -- the L-064-9 class; re-staged so the doc was in R2.) R2 PASS, all 9 areas, no findings. Full ai_router pytest suite GREEN: 1767 passed / 1 pre-existing skip (baseline 1736 + 31 new across both test files). Metered verification ~$0.473 (R1 $0.276 + R2 $0.197) + next-session rec $0.003. NO release this session (PyPI is S6); ai_router version unchanged; NO Marketplace/extension change (spec non-goal: no UI this whole set). DEFERRED to later sessions: the probe-template lane (S3), the Podman model-authored-probe lane gated on a green spike (S4), the ceiling->floor ratchet + measured replacement gate (S5), the release + dogfood (S6). Next-session rec routed (next-session-rec.md): anthropic/opus-4-8/medium for S3's probe-template-lane coding (a non-experimental shared-adapter coding session where first-pass correctness dominates total cost; the analysis model flagged its own stale model-name mapping).

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (next-session-rec.md) recommends anthropic/opus-4-8 for S3's probe-template-lane coding: a non-experimental shared-adapter coding session (pull_verifier/pull_critique + a new template-lane module) where first-pass correctness dominates total cost over token price (S2's R1 FAIL underlined the rework risk). Same engine as S2, effort dialed to medium. Operator may instead pick codex gpt-5.4/high or gemini-2.5-pro; cross-provider verification stays REQUIRED (the diff will trip routed_gate).
